hook.text: JavaScript makes the web interactive. The engine is the brain that runs it — fast, compiled, and optimized.

buildup.text: V8 (Chrome), SpiderMonkey (Firefox), and JavaScriptCore (Safari) compile JS to machine code at runtime.

discovery.text: JIT compilation profiles hot code paths and optimizes them. Cold paths run interpreted for quick startup.

twist.text: Garbage collection reclaims unused memory. Pauses used to freeze pages. Modern GCs work incrementally.

climax.text: V8 powers Node.js too. The same engine that runs your browser also runs server-side JavaScript.

punchline.text: JS engines JIT-compile code at runtime. Fast enough for apps.

quiz.question: What technique do modern JS engines use to maximize performance?
options: JIT compilation that optimizes hot code paths
Pre-compiling all JavaScript before the page loads
Running JavaScript in a separate thread by default
